item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Confirmation by the artist that the images have been released under CC-BY-SA 3-0 has been acquired.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Fix implemented, as it makes sense per the definitions of the words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-1554-7 terminated due to a lack of oxygen in the testing chamber.

Real nitpicky, but shouldn't it be expired since the Foundation didn't kill him, he just died due to lack of oxygen?

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

Hmm, this is a tricky one. A lot of people have been complaining about the tacked-on gravity self-defence, so maybe I could link it to another skip that uses gravity as self-defence? Maybe SCP-2919?

Editing this:

Following this, D-1554-7 was thrown against the northern wall of the testing unit, and reported severe difficulty moving and breathing as SCP-1554 continued to burn.

To this:

Following this, D-1554-7 was thrown1 against the northern wall of the testing unit, and reported severe difficulty moving and breathing as SCP-1554 continued to burn.

Footnotes
1. SCP-1554 appeared to use gravity as a self-defence measure in a similar manner to SCP-2919. A link between these two anomalies has yet to be established.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I, uh, think they meant "cutting". As in, taking a cutting of a plant and replanting it to grow separately.

Also, they said it three years ago, I doubt they're keeping up with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gee whiz, I wonder what could possibly happen if you cut a page vs. if you're cutting it? I just wonder what!?!?!?!?!?!?

idiot.png
facepalm.png

Edit: OK, maybe I was a little too sassy there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1554 was ignited due to a cigarette lighter smuggled into the testing chamber by D-1554-7, a known pyromaniac.

-1 only for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with whoever wrote that card.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is basically the opposite of dada terrorism and I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1. Gravity part felt a bit out of place, but the notion of the pages "re-enacting" parts of LotR was genuinely interesting.

What happens when a large deal of them are damaged? We may never know as this is the only instance of SCP-1554 known to the Foundation at this time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it, +1.

The effect doesn't seem to match the level of aggression in the note. The manifetations don't seem to be particularly hostile or dangerous (except for the gravitation). The bibliophile who wrote that note sounded like he was aiming for the book to have a self-defense mechanism more than anything else. Maybe he tried and fell short?

The researchers apparantly missed a vital research opportunity. They could have finally answered the question that has plagued man for ages; did the balrog have wings?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, shiiiit. Someone tore out the whole Bombadil chapter. This means that there's a Tom Bombadil out there somewhere…

(Why does my iPad correct Bombadil to Bombadildo? What the hell?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The gravity part still feels tacked on. It'd be much improved if that was just cut entirely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: By golly, I think you are on to something here!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I keep coming back to this one and can't decide why it doesn't grab me. I love books and I adore Tolkien, but nothing about this concept or the images really stands out for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: PM me first to make sure I'm okay with the syntax changes you're doing; I don't want the actual meaning of sentences to be changed, but if they can flow any better, I'm cool with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A few little nitpicks — I think the article was a bit repetitive in some places, and if I may make some minor syntax edits, it'd clear that right up — but nothing that would stop me from +1ing :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It kinda does, but now it reminds me of SCP-1136 except it's a book instead of a gecko. Retagging accordingly.

Also, just a minor nitpick: You mention that the D-class was "terminated" by lack of oxygen. Terminated typically means intentionally offing someone, and in this case he was merely collateral damage in the process of protecting a fragile SCP object. "D-#### suffocated due to lack of oxygen" would work just as well, methinks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…well, shit.

Guess I'm rewriting this, then.

EDIT: Redid the gravity aspect. Is the tag applicable now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I removed the gravity tag because it simply doesn't make sense. If it flings the offender into the air without causing everything /else/ around them to be thrown upwards as well (including the book itself), it's merely a telekinetic force. Not to mention a mere change in gravity wouldn't suddenly cause the site's building structure to suddenly adopt the strength of foam board.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I rather enjoyed this article, particularly the note at the end. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if you cut a page? Does this produce similar results to cutting?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will now copy-paste a quote and put up a '+1' sign without contributing anything useful to the discussion.

if you ruin ONE MORE FUCKING PAGE there will be consequences

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: well, it's funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of the anomalous bureaucrats who created SCP-1047.

Sign thieves get what they deserve.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I generally wait until the end of an article before I upvote, but I think I tried to upvote this every time I got to a new description of what happens when you screw with the book. Yes, I love Tolkien. And honestly, the gravity effect is awesome. The largest possible threat to any paper tends to be fire, given it would envelope the entire work . This is simply a book making sure that whoever is aiming for permanent and quick destruction instead receives such a fate themself. Enthusiastic upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I downvoted at first because we've already got a book carving SCP, but the more I read the test logs, the more I liked it. The part about extinguishing the Balrog creating more -A's got a laugh out of me. So I think this is a worthy entry after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's funny because the end result of this was basically encouraging people to vandalize the book instead of getting people to leave it alone. I love books, but I'd probably play around with this if I could.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty nifty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The gravity tag was a bitch to integrate into this, and if people object enough, I may take it down to revise that particular part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it! Very interesting idea (to my knowledge although it sounds familiar, I don't think the territory has been explored much before), and you certainly got the artistic and adaptive tags I'd say. I might also be a little influenced by my inherent love of Tolkien's universe and his written works, but what better book to physically transform into representations of its own scenes than one with that much description and depiction? Certainly fits the bill.
I would say however (just to be niggly) the last part of the description where:
"burning the pages of SCP-1554 causes a sudden gravitational shift of approximately ███ G in an upward direction, invariably resulting in the death of the individual who ignited SCP-1554."
is a little bit vague and looks like you've just thrown it in to make up for your "gravity" tag. While it might be good to integrate it a little more with the rest of the description, or optionally make the gravity thing a little more involved with the main subject of the book's anomalousness; I would suggest maybe a more detailed description of the sudden gravitational force, for example:
Does it just happen in a specific measurable region near or around the book? Does it deliberately target the person who set it on fire, or just blindly cause the shift to its surroundings?
Just a thought. Otherwise, great job!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my entry for Wilt's challenge. The three tags I got were artistic, adaptive, and gravity. Hopefully this holds up.
